guest_credentials: Professional gravel racer; multiple gravel race winner including Unbound and Gravel Art Series Rift; also competes in road cycling and works on PhD research
key_claims: Gravel training should be 80% polarized: 80% easy, 20% hard, focusing on V2 max sessions and fatmax intervals rather than road training plans
V2 max block structure: 2 days of high-intensity V2 max intervals (e.g., 4x8min at 106% FTP or 3x10x40/20s) followed by 1 recovery day, repeated 3-4 times for a total 2-week block
Uses lactate testing to determine FTP and interval intensities rather than 20-minute or 1-hour tests; adjusts intervals based on first and second inflection point readings

Fatmax training is essential for gravel because the surface resistance prevents full recovery, requiring athletes to spend significant time in a zone that's uncomfortable but doesn't allow complete recovery

Altitude training at 1900m in Andorra provides measurable benefits (5, 10, and 20-minute power PRs) when maintained for 2-3 weeks; trains high, sleeps high, but descends to 1300m for above-threshold intervals

Heat training extended altitude benefits for 8-10 days; used active on-bike heat training (ramping core temperature to 38.2-38.4°C) to extend altitude performance effect rather than passive sauna for adaptation
Gravel racing demands very high average power due to limited drafting opportunities; nutrition strategy critical—plan to consume 10-20 grams more carbs per hour than baseline capacity

Tire choice matters significantly more than power loss: using maximum tire clearance (45mm vs 40mm slicks) on technical terrain allows 45-second+ faster descents through delayed braking and increased cornering confidence
specific_numbers: 2-week V2 max block with 6 hit workouts and 3 recovery days (9 days total)
Training down to 1300m for above-threshold intervals
2-2.5 weeks minimum altitude exposure for measurable effect
Core temperature ramped to 38.2-38.4°C in active heat training
Rift race: ~7-7.5 hour effort, 200km distance, attacked after 30km and rode 170km solo
European Championships: 40km solo effort with 2.5+ minute gap, 50m rear tire flat on final descent
Training load of 25 hours per week in priority B race blocks
1.5-2 hour warm-up rides day before priority races
cluster_reasoning: Rosa's episode is fundamentally about training methodology: V2max intervals, lactate testing, polarized training structure, altitude training protocols, and periodization—all core coaching content. Nutrition and recovery are secondary because they support the primary training framework.
